subject: Re: Boxnet loading extremely slow
body:
Hi!

I also see the same problem with Warp 1.0.9.  Loading the BoxNet models 
takes 30 minutes or longer, regardless of model.  This is on a Windows 10 
machine with a Geforce RTX 3080 Ti, 128Gb RAM, and an Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU 
E5-2630 0 @ 2.30GHz.  I would love to see this time reduced.  Please let us 
know whether if this is normal behaviour or an issue that needs resolving.

Cheers,

Stefan 

On Wednesday, February 24, 2021 at 7:58:02 AM UTC-8 tom.de...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hi teige, 
>
> I am seeing the exact same behaviour on our new machine with Geforce RTX 
> 3090 cards. have you been able to solve this by any chance?
>
> Many thanks,
> Tom
>
> Op vrijdag 5 februari 2021 om 09:12:19 UTC schreef teig...@gmail.com:
>
>> Hi Warpers, 
>>
>> Search brought up a couple previous similar messages but no helpful info, 
>> and I guess this might not be an easy fix but I’ll try just in case: 
>> On a Windows 10 workstation with Geforce RTX 3090, Warp 1.0.9, 
>> BoxNet2Mask_20180531 from public repository 
>> Loading the boxnet model, either upon start processing, or upon first 
>> test picking one micrograph, takes a very long time, >10 minutes, with the 
>> “loading boxnet model” message hanging, and Windows periodically declaring 
>> Warp is not responding, then recovering in ~1 minute cycles. 
>>
>> Is this a bug others have had and can I provide more info to make this 
>> more useful? 
>>
>> All the best 
>> Teige
>
>
